From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian actress and voice actress

| Andreina Pagnani | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | Andreina Gentili  (1906-11-24)24 November 1906  [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), [Italy](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Italy "Kingdom of Italy") |
| Died | 22 November 1981(1981-11-22) (aged 74)  [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), Italy |
| Occupations | * Actress * voice actress |
| Years active | 1924–1981 |
| Spouse | Federico Franco Pagnani  ​  ​ (m. 1927; died 1933)​ |

**Andreina Pagnani** (born **Andreina Gentili**; 24 November 1906 – 22 November 1981) was an Italian actress and voice actress.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andreina_Pagnani&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Born in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), Italy, and the daughter of a [dresser](/wiki/Dresser_(theatre) "Dresser (theatre)"), at a young age Pagnani studied the harp and the piano, then she devoted herself to theatre and then entering the stage companies Artistica Operaia e Giovanni Emanuel.[[1]](#cite_note-bio-1) In a short time Pagnani became a *[prima donna](/wiki/Prima_donna "Prima donna")* of Italian theater, working among others with [Totò](/wiki/Tot%C3%B2 "Totò"), [Gino Cervi](/wiki/Gino_Cervi "Gino Cervi"), [Gabriele Ferzetti](/wiki/Gabriele_Ferzetti "Gabriele Ferzetti"), [Lauretta Masiero](/wiki/Lauretta_Masiero "Lauretta Masiero").[[1]](#cite_note-bio-1)

She made her film debut in 1924, in the silent film *L'osteria di Mozzadita*.[[1]](#cite_note-bio-1) Her film activity was long but very irregular, and almost entirely consisting of supporting roles.[[1]](#cite_note-bio-1) She obtained a large popularity as the [Maigret](/wiki/Jules_Maigret "Jules Maigret")'s wife in the TV series *[Le inchieste del commissario Maigret](/wiki/Le_inchieste_del_commissario_Maigret "Le inchieste del commissario Maigret")*, broadcast on [RAI](/wiki/RAI "RAI") from 1964 to 1972.[[1]](#cite_note-bio-1)

Pagnani was also very active as a voice actress and a dubber.[[2]](#cite_note-2) She dubbed actresses such as [Marlene Dietrich](/wiki/Marlene_Dietrich "Marlene Dietrich"), [Bette Davis](/wiki/Bette_Davis "Bette Davis"), [Rhonda Fleming](/wiki/Rhonda_Fleming "Rhonda Fleming"), [Gloria Swanson](/wiki/Gloria_Swanson "Gloria Swanson"), [Katharine Hepburn](/wiki/Katharine_Hepburn "Katharine Hepburn"), [Barbara Stanwyck](/wiki/Barbara_Stanwyck "Barbara Stanwyck"), [Greta Garbo](/wiki/Greta_Garbo "Greta Garbo"), [Ginger Rogers](/wiki/Ginger_Rogers "Ginger Rogers") and many more. She was among the earliest of Italy's prominent dub actresses along with [Lydia Simoneschi](/wiki/Lydia_Simoneschi "Lydia Simoneschi"), [Rosetta Calavetta](/wiki/Rosetta_Calavetta "Rosetta Calavetta"), [Giovanna Scotto](/wiki/Giovanna_Scotto "Giovanna Scotto") and [Tina Lattanzi](/wiki/Tina_Lattanzi "Tina Lattanzi").

### Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andreina_Pagnani&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 1927, Pagnani was married to the aviator Federico Franco Pagnani until he died in a plane crash in 1933. She also dated actor [Alberto Sordi](/wiki/Alberto_Sordi "Alberto Sordi") for nine years, who was nearly 14 years her junior.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andreina_Pagnani&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Filmography")]

### Cinema

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andreina_Pagnani&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Cinema")]

* *Mese mariano* (1929)
* *Patatrac* (1931)
* *Acqua cheta* (1933) – Anita
* *Il presidente della Ba.Ce.Cre.Mi.* (1933) – Signorina Rossi
* *[The Little Schoolmistress](/wiki/The_Little_Schoolmistress_(1934_film) "The Little Schoolmistress (1934 film)")* (1934) – Maria Bini, la maestrina
* *Quella vecchia canaglia* (1934) – Susanna
* *Il serpente a sonagli* (1935) – Sonia
* *Orizzonte dipinto* (1941)
* *[Apparition](/wiki/Apparition_(1943_film) "Apparition (1943 film)")* (1943) – Zia Ortensia
* *Caccia all'uomo* (1948) – Suor Simplicia
* *[La nemica](/wiki/La_nemica "La nemica")* (1952) – Duchessa Anna di Nemi (Voice, Uncredited)
* *[Concert of Intrigue](/wiki/Concert_of_Intrigue "Concert of Intrigue")* (1954) – Elisabetta Tabor || (Voice, Uncredited)
* *[Il padrone sono me](/wiki/Il_padrone_sono_me "Il padrone sono me")* (1955) – La signora Maria
* *Io, Caterina* (1957)
* *Domenica è sempre domenica* (1958) – Countess Clotilde Landolfi Ferretti
* *[Call Girls of Rome](/wiki/Call_Girls_of_Rome "Call Girls of Rome")* (1960) – Arabella
* *[Le pillole di Ercole](/wiki/Le_pillole_di_Ercole "Le pillole di Ercole")* (1960) – Giovanna
* *[The Lovemakers](/wiki/The_Lovemakers_(film) "The Lovemakers (film)")* (1961) – Maîtresse (Voice, Uncredited)
* *[The Last Judgment](/wiki/The_Last_Judgment_(1961_film) "The Last Judgment (1961 film)")* (1961) – Guest of Matteoni
* *[The Commandant](/wiki/The_Commandant_(film) "The Commandant (film)")* (1963) – Francesca Cavalli
* *I 2 vigili* (1967) – Herself (Uncredited)
